# Activity Lecture 6 {#sec-week2}

Name:

1.  The most common blood type in the United States is O+, with around 38% of the population having this type of blood. Consider taking a random sample of size 4. Let Y denote the number of people in the sample with type O+. Find

    1.  P(Y=0)

    2.  P(Y=2)

    3.  P(0\<Y\<=2)

2.  Neuroblastoma is a rare but treatable disease. A urine test has been developed that gives a positive diagnostic in about 70% of cases with the disease. Assume that a group of people will be tested, of whom 8 have the disease.

    1.  What is the probability that all 8 cases will be detected?

    2.  What is the probability that only one case will be missed?

    3.  What is the probability that two or more cases will be missed?

    4.  What is the expected number of people with the disease?

    5.  What is the standard deviation?
